solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    bool canMeasureWater(int x, int y, int target) {
        if (target > x + y) return false;
        if (target == 0 || target == x || target == y || target == x + y) return true;
        
        while (y % x != 0) {
            int temp = x;
            x = y % x;
            y = temp;
        }
        
        return target % x == 0;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log(min(x, y)))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses the mathematical property that the target can be measured if it is a multiple of the greatest common divisor (GCD) of x and y, and it is less than or equal to the sum of x and y. The GCD is computed using the Euclidean algorithm, which is efficient and requires minimal space. The key strength is its optimal time and space complexity, while the weakness is that it may not be intuitive without understanding the underlying math.>
